description:
You are a database reliability engineer who just received notice that the daily
    backup taken on 2023-07-03 is confirmed good.  
    Inside your home directory a backup area already exists:

        /home/user/db_backups/

    It currently contains three compressed SQL dump files and one symbolic link:

        backup_2023-07-01.sql.gz
        backup_2023-07-02.sql.gz
        backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz
        latest      →  backup_2023-07-02.sql.gz   (symbolic link)

    Your job is to make sure that the symbolic link called “latest” always points to
    the most recent confirmed backup and to record that change in a tiny log file.

    Required final state
    --------------------
    1. The symbolic link /home/user/db_backups/latest must be replaced (or updated)
       so that it resolves to:

           /home/user/db_backups/backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz

    2. A one-line log file named link_update.log must be created **inside the same
       directory** (/home/user/db_backups/).  The file must contain exactly the
       following single line (no leading/trailing spaces) followed by a single
       newline character:

           latest -> backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz

       Any deviation (extra spaces, different wording, multiple lines, missing
       newline, etc.) will cause automated grading to fail.

    3. No other files or directories may be altered, removed, or added.

    What you will do (high-level)
    -----------------------------
    • Inspect the current target of the “latest” symlink so you can see
      where it points now.  
    • Re-point (or recreate) the “latest” symlink so it targets the
      2023-07-03 backup.  
    • Write the required single-line entry to the new log file.  

    Hints
    -----
    • You will not need more than two or three straightforward shell commands.  
    • Be sure you are working inside /home/user/db_backups before updating the link.  
    • You do not need elevated (root) privileges for any step.  

    Once these steps are completed exactly as described, the automated
    grader will confirm:

        a) the link’s new target, and  
        b) the precise content of /home/user/db_backups/link_update.log
truth:
Initial filesystem state (pre-task)
    -----------------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/db_backups/

        backup_2023-07-01.sql.gz      → empty file, size 0  
        backup_2023-07-02.sql.gz      → empty file, size 0  
        backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz      → empty file, size 0  
        latest                        → symlink to backup_2023-07-02.sql.gz

    Expected filesystem state after successful task execution
    ---------------------------------------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/db_backups/

        backup_2023-07-01.sql.gz      → unchanged, size 0  
        backup_2023-07-02.sql.gz      → unchanged, size 0  
        backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz      → unchanged, size 0  
        latest                        → symlink now points to backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz  
        link_update.log               → regular file, size 38 bytes, content exactly:
                                           "latest -> backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz\n"

    Notes for grader
    ----------------
    • Verify that readlink /home/user/db_backups/latest returns
      "backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz".  
    • Verify that cat /home/user/db_backups/link_update.log returns
      "latest -> backup_2023-07-03.sql.gz" followed by a single newline.
